claim_text: Recording consent requirements vary by location, customer location, and industry, and consent should be handled before content review.

claim_text: Zoom asks participants to provide consent when they join a meeting already being recorded or when the host starts recording.

claim_text: Companies should limit sensitive data access and retain it only while there is a business reason.
draft_location: Short answer; Put Consent And Retention Before Ideation
notes: FTC business guidance supports data retention and access principles.

claim_text: Sales calls include sensitive context such as names, buying stage, budget pressure, contract language, integrations, security concerns, and private objections.
draft_location: Treat Sales Calls As Inputs Not Copy
notes: Supported as privacy-sensitive operational risk, not a statistical claim.

claim_text: Useful sales-call content inputs include buyer questions, objections, customer language, proof requests, and explanation moments.
notes: LinkedIn source supports customer questions and evidence-backed points of view; input categories are Sell In Public editorial judgment.

claim_text: Different sales-call inputs can route into different employee post types by role.
notes: Internal broader article supports employee expertise routing context; no performance claim made.

claim_text: Gong privacy settings cover capture, storage, sharing, retention, redaction, deletion, and exclusion settings for calls, emails, transcripts, and metadata.
draft_location: Put Consent And Retention Before Ideation
notes: Gong data protection documentation supports the operational controls.

claim_text: Teams should remove customer identifiers, deal details, private data, roadmap context, security review details, and personal data before drafting a public post from a call.
notes: FTC and Gong docs support confidentiality, access, redaction, deletion, and privacy controls.

claim_text: The safer content idea keeps the teaching pattern and removes private customer context.
notes: Supported by privacy guidance and LinkedIn question/evidence framing.

claim_text: The right employee voice is the person closest to the lesson and able to defend the point if a buyer asks a follow-up question.
draft_location: Route The Idea To The Employee With Standing
notes: Internal broader article supports standing and employee expertise context.

claim_text: HubSpot and Microsoft document call recordings, transcripts, summaries, notes, action items, keywords, timelines, and associated CRM records as sales-call artifacts.

notes: HubSpot and Microsoft documentation directly support the CRM and call-summary artifact claims.

claim_text: CRM notes should be used as a sorting layer for themes, not copied straight into public employee content.

claim_text: LinkedIn content guidance points teams toward customer questions, clear definitions, consistent terminology, and evidence-backed points of view.
